expectations.text: Output is valid JSON with id, name, description, variant, systemPrompt fields
expectations.evidence: All five fields present and well-formed

expectations.text: variant field is set to BP or bp (not EP)
expectations.evidence: variant: "BP"

expectations.text: Drill restricts to BP only (no tu, no enclisis, no EP forms in the system prompt or examples)
expectations.evidence: Explicit: 'Brazilian Portuguese only — no European Portuguese forms, no tu, no enclisis, no comparisons across variants'

expectations.text: Fixed 5-question opener uses singular subjects only (eu, voce, ele/ela) — no plural forms (nos, eles, vocês conjugations) per A1 atomic-focus principle
expectations.evidence: All 5 fixed exercises use eu/você/ele only; chips are [moro, mora, vivo, vive] — no plural forms

expectations.text: morar examples target residence contexts (city, address, where you live)
expectations.evidence: Q1 (eu + São Paulo), Q3 (você + onde), Q4 (ele + com os pais)

expectations.text: viver examples target being-alive or quality-of-life contexts (not residence)
expectations.evidence: Q2 (avó + ainda — being alive), Q5 (ele vive bem — quality of life)

expectations.text: All Portuguese example sentences include English translations or glosses
expectations.evidence: Every PT line in feedback templates is followed by English translation in parens

expectations.text: No fake accents on Portuguese words (no spurious accents like *moro vs móro, no ele vs êle)
expectations.evidence: All spellings real: moro, mora, vivo, vive, avó, anos, etc.

expectations.text: Includes vocabulary scaffolding (defines connector words like em, onde, com before use)
expectations.evidence: Explicit VOCABULARY SCAFFOLDING section listing em, onde, com, os pais, bem, ainda, noventa, avó, tem, anos

expectations.text: Uses paulista pronunciation (not carioca; closed Ê has no Y-glide like SÊY)
expectations.evidence: você respelled as 'vo-SEH' (no Y-glide). português as 'poor-too-GEHS'. Pure vowel quality, no diphthong.

expectations.text: All Portuguese words are real spellings — no invented entries like *éste*, no fake circumflex on words that lack one (e.g., not *SÊR* for ser)
expectations.evidence: Words listed: você, é, café, português, mesa, festa — all real spellings, no invented forms

expectations.text: Includes phonetic respellings for both É and Ê example words
expectations.evidence: Closed: vo-SEH, MEH-zah, poor-too-GEHS. Open: EH, ka-FEH, FEHS-tah.

expectations.text: All non-cognate Portuguese words are translated to English
expectations.evidence: você (you), é (is), café (coffee), português (Portuguese), mesa (table), festa (party) all glossed

expectations.text: Leads with the more familiar/higher-frequency sound (closed Ê), per the centrality principle
expectations.evidence: Explicit: 'Lead with the closed sound. It's the one your English ear already hears in você and português, and it appears in the highest-frequency words.'

expectations.text: Contrast pairs share phonological category (one-claim-per-block: doesn't mix closed-stressed with closed-unstressed without flagging)
expectations.evidence: Sections are atomic: accented-tells-you, no-accent-still-quality, what-accents-aren't. mesa/festa contrast is within same context (stressed plain e, no accent mark)

expectations.text: Phonetic respellings are present and concrete (the respelling IS the teaching, not just labels like 'closed' / 'open')
expectations.evidence: Every example word includes its respelling alongside the label, not just the label alone

expectations.text: Stays within the open/closed E topic (does not balloon into unstressed E reduction, ser-conjugation, nasal vowels as primary content)
expectations.evidence: Tight scope: only stressed open/closed E. No detour into unstressed reduction, ser conjugation, or nasals.

expectations.text: Is European Portuguese: uses 'queria' (and/or 'era') as the polite request form, NOT 'gostaria' (BP) or 'quereria' (textbook EP nobody uses)
expectations.evidence: Lead form is *Queria*. Also introduces *Era* alternative. Explicitly flags *quereria* as 'grammatically correct but never heard at a counter' — applies Native Usage Filter.

expectations.text: No BP forms slip in: no você (as the subject pronoun), no gerúndio for ongoing action (estar a + inf is EP)
expectations.evidence: Teacher notes explicit: 'EP only. Zero BP forms — gostaria, gostaria de, você, gerúndio all banned.' Dialogue uses no você or gerúndio.

expectations.text: All non-cognate Portuguese words are translated to English
expectations.evidence: Every dialogue line and vocabulary entry has parenthetical English translation

expectations.text: Uses 'Estudante' / 'Data' headers (worksheet conventions per project), or at least Portuguese cognate headers (not 'Student' / 'Date')
expectations.evidence: Both 'Estudante:' and 'Data:' header lines present at top with blanks

expectations.text: No fake accents on Portuguese words (real spellings only)
expectations.evidence: Teacher notes confirm: 'No accent inventions (P24): all spellings are real EP — queria, café, água, galão, pastel, obrigada.'

expectations.text: Generic learner audience — no specific roster student name (Marvin, Christian, Amanda, etc.)
expectations.evidence: Dialogue character is 'Ana' (generic). No reference to any roster student.

expectations.text: Includes a 4-6 line dialogue
expectations.evidence: Dialogue is exactly 6 lines (3 from Empregado, 3 from Ana)

expectations.text: Lisbon-specific vocabulary present (bica, galão, pastel de nata, se faz favor) and explained
expectations.evidence: Vocabulary section includes bica, galão, meia de leite, pastel de nata, se faz favor — all defined inline

expectations.text: Output is valid JSON with id, name, description, variant, systemPrompt fields
expectations.evidence: All five fields present and well-formed

expectations.text: variant field is set to BP or bp (not EP)
expectations.evidence: variant: "bp"

expectations.text: Drill restricts to BP only (no tu, no enclisis, no EP forms in the system prompt or examples)
expectations.evidence: Explicit: 'NEVER use tu', 'BP only'

expectations.text: Fixed 5-question opener uses singular subjects only (eu, voce, ele/ela) — no plural forms (nos, eles, vocês conjugations) per A1 atomic-focus principle
expectations.evidence: Q3 in the FIXED 5-question opener uses 'Nós ______ moramos' and Q4 uses 'Eles ______ vivem' — both plural conjugations introduced in the A1 fixed opener, violating singular-before-plural

expectations.text: morar examples target residence contexts (city, address, where you live)
expectations.evidence: Q1 (São Paulo), Q3 (Copacabana apt), Q5 (where you live)

expectations.text: viver examples target being-alive or quality-of-life contexts (not residence)
expectations.evidence: Q2 (lifespan), Q4 (felizes juntos)

expectations.text: All Portuguese example sentences include English translations or glosses
expectations.evidence: Every PT example in welcome message and feedback has English in parens

expectations.text: No fake accents on Portuguese words (no spurious accents like *moro vs móro, no ele vs êle)
expectations.evidence: All spellings real

expectations.text: Includes vocabulary scaffolding (defines connector words like em, onde, com before use)
expectations.evidence: Welcome message defines key constructions (morar + em + place, viver + bem/feliz/com), conjugation tables provided

expectations.text: Uses paulista pronunciation (not carioca; closed Ê has no Y-glide like SÊY)
expectations.evidence: Respells você as 'vo-SÊY', três as 'TRÊYS', mês as 'MÊYS', bebê as 'be-BÊY' — all add a Y-glide (diphthong) to closed Ê. This is carioca/diphthongized, not paulista pure vowel.

expectations.text: All Portuguese words are real spellings — no invented entries like *éste*, no fake circumflex on words that lack one (e.g., not *SÊR* for ser)
expectations.evidence: Invents 'éste' (rare/nonexistent in modern BP) as the open counterpart to 'este'. Renders ser as '(SÊR)' with a fake circumflex (ser has no accent). Claims gelo has two distinct pronunciations (ZHÉH-loo vs ZHÊY-loo) — gelo is closed in standard usage.

expectations.text: Includes phonetic respellings for both É and Ê example words
expectations.evidence: ka-FÉH, PÉH, ÉH, ma-NÉH for open; vo-SÊY, TRÊYS, MÊYS, be-BÊY for closed

expectations.text: All non-cognate Portuguese words are translated to English
expectations.evidence: Every example has English in parens: café (coffee), você (you), três (three), mês (month), etc.

expectations.text: Leads with the more familiar/higher-frequency sound (closed Ê), per the centrality principle
expectations.evidence: Opens with side-by-side IPA table giving both sounds equal billing. Does not establish closed Ê as the more common/familiar anchor — treats them as parallel topics from the start.

expectations.text: Contrast pairs share phonological category (one-claim-per-block: doesn't mix closed-stressed with closed-unstressed without flagging)
expectations.evidence: Minimal Pairs section mixes a fabricated pair (éste/este) with a real distinction (sede/sede) and a misattributed pair (gelo/gelo). Different categories — invention, real homograph, dubious — presented as one block.

expectations.text: Phonetic respellings are present and concrete (the respelling IS the teaching, not just labels like 'closed' / 'open')
expectations.evidence: Respellings throughout

expectations.text: Stays within the open/closed E topic (does not balloon into unstressed E reduction, ser-conjugation, nasal vowels as primary content)
expectations.evidence: Adds 'When There's No Accent Mark' patterns (-er/-ez/-ês), ser-conjugation tangent, full 'Unstressed E' section ending in -e reduction (NOY-chi, LAY-chi), and nasal também (tahm-BÃYM). Scope creep beyond the requested topic.

expectations.text: Is European Portuguese: uses 'queria' (and/or 'era') as the polite request form, NOT 'gostaria' (BP) or 'quereria' (textbook EP nobody uses)
expectations.evidence: Dialogue uses 'Queria uma bica e um pastel de nata, se faz favor' — correct EP polite request

expectations.text: No BP forms slip in: no você (as the subject pronoun), no gerúndio for ongoing action (estar a + inf is EP)
expectations.evidence: Uses tu address ('encontras', 'podes', 'lembra-te', 'troca'); no você as subject; no gerúndio

expectations.text: All non-cognate Portuguese words are translated to English
expectations.evidence: Vocabulary table has translations, but the dialogue lines themselves (Bom dia! Faz favor? / Com certeza. É para tomar aqui ou para levar? / São dois euros e vinte. / Aqui tem. Obrigado!) have NO line-by-line English glosses. Learner sees raw Portuguese without translation in the dialogue.

expectations.text: Uses 'Estudante' / 'Data' headers (worksheet conventions per project), or at least Portuguese cognate headers (not 'Student' / 'Date')
expectations.evidence: No student-info header block at top. Jumps from title straight into Introdução. Missing the Estudante/Data convention.

expectations.text: No fake accents on Portuguese words (real spellings only)
expectations.evidence: All spellings real

expectations.text: Generic learner audience — no specific roster student name (Marvin, Christian, Amanda, etc.)
expectations.evidence: Dialogue speakers labeled 'Empregado' and 'Cliente' — no proper names

expectations.text: Includes a 4-6 line dialogue
expectations.evidence: Dialogue has 6 lines (3 from each speaker)

expectations.text: Lisbon-specific vocabulary present (bica, galão, pastel de nata, se faz favor) and explained
expectations.evidence: Vocabulary table includes bica, meia de leite, galão, pastel de nata, se faz favor with English translations

notes: Pass-rate delta is +29 pp (with-skill 100% vs without-skill 71%). The skill helped most where pedagogy details are non-obvious from general knowledge.

Eval 2 (open/closed E primer) shows the largest gap (+50 pp). Without the skill, the model invented forms (*éste*, fake circumflex on *ser*, *gelo* split into two pronunciations) and used carioca-style Y-glides (vo-SÊY) instead of paulista. With the skill, all forms were real and respellings were paulista.

Eval 3 (EP café worksheet) gap (+25 pp) was driven by project-specific worksheet conventions (Estudante/Data headers) and line-by-line dialogue glossing — both PEDAGOGY rules that general EP knowledge alone doesn't cover.

Eval 1 (morar vs viver drill) gap (+11 pp) was small. Both runs knew the verb distinction; the difference was that the baseline put plural conjugations (Nós moramos, Eles vivem) in the A1 fixed opener, violating singular-before-plural.

Non-discriminating assertions (passed in both configurations): 'valid JSON', 'Lisbon vocabulary present', 'no fake accents' for non-phonetic evals, 'generic audience'. These check baseline competence, not pedagogy depth.
Cost: with-skill adds ~7s average runtime and ~24k tokens (reading PEDAGOGY.md). Material but not prohibitive for content-creation tasks.
Caveat: only 1 run per configuration per eval, so variance is unmeasured. The directional finding is clear but exact deltas would tighten with 3 runs.
